channel.title: The Genome Brief
channel.description: A daily ~3-minute brief on viral clinical & wastewater genomics, biotech/pharma business news, AI in biomedicine, and a fun animal story to close.
channel.itunes:author: Nigel

item.title: PanRes structure-aware AMR database adds 35% wastewater resistome hits + Pfizer's Seagen-derived ADC sigvotatug vedotin misses Phase 3 OS in NSCLC + Merck's tulisokibart is first anti-TL1A to clear Phase 3 (UC) + Arrepath ML-guided antibiotic HTS doubles hit rate + Australian ballista spider's prey-triggered snare
item.description: PanRes (Munk lab, DTU) unifies 11,717 AMR genes into 598 structurally conserved clusters and uses 3D-based HMMs to expand wastewater resistome detection by 35.2% beyond high-confidence BLAST across seven European cities (bioRxiv, 22 June 2026). A Kinshasa serosurvey of 1,250 dried-blood-spots puts dengue IgG at 38.1% and chikungunya at 24.2%, with time-varying force-of-infection modeling (medRxiv, 22 June 2026). Pfizer's sigvotatug vedotin, the first new ADC from the $43B Seagen deal, misses overall survival in Phase 3 SigVie-002 versus docetaxel in second-line non-squamous NSCLC; pembrolizumab combo Phase 3 ongoing (Pfizer, 23 June 2026). Merck's tulisokibart (ex-Prometheus, $10.8B 2023) becomes the first anti-TL1A to hit a Phase 3, clearing primary and key secondary endpoints in the ATLAS-UC induction study in moderate-to-severe UC (Merck, 22 June 2026). Arrepath's ML-guided phenotypic HTS against E. coli (incl. hyperpermeable lptD- mutant) reports a doubled hit rate and 3x fewer toxic hits versus conventional, with robust performance on structurally novel cherry-picked compounds (bioRxiv, 22 June 2026). And the ballista spider (Propostira sp., Narendra lab, Macquarie) builds a prey-triggered spring-loaded snare for the green tree ant Oecophylla smaragdina, launching its catch at >1,300 m/s² (~140 g) — Current Biology, 22 June 2026.
item.itunes:summary: PanRes (Munk lab, DTU) — structure-aware AMR database with 11,717 genes and 3D HMMs — adds 35.2% additional wastewater resistome hits beyond high-confidence BLAST across seven European cities (bioRxiv, 22 June 2026). Kinshasa serosurvey: dengue IgG 38.1%, chikungunya 24.2% in 1,250 adults — under-appreciated arboviral burden in central Africa (medRxiv, 22 June 2026). Pfizer's sigvotatug vedotin, the first new Seagen-derived ADC, misses OS in Phase 3 SigVie-002 in 2L non-squamous NSCLC (Pfizer, 23 June 2026). Merck's tulisokibart (ex-Prometheus) is the first anti-TL1A in Phase 3 history to hit primary + key secondary endpoints — week-12 clinical remission in moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis (ATLAS-UC induction, Merck, 22 June 2026). Arrepath's ML-guided phenotypic antibiotic HTS against E. coli doubles hit rate and yields 3x fewer toxic hits than conventional screens (bioRxiv, 22 June 2026). And in Current Biology: a new Australian ballista spider that builds a prey-triggered spring-loaded silk snare for one ant species, accelerating its catch at >140 g (Narendra lab, Macquarie, 22 June 2026).

item.title: Bundibugyo Ebola passes 1,000 confirmed cases in DRC + Maputo severe malaria HIV coinfection pfkelch signal + AbbVie's $10.9B Apogee buy for IL-13 antibody zumilokibart + Insilico/SK Biopharm $2.5B AI deal + biomeStat agentic AI for dengue genomics + Heliconius butterflies barely age
item.description: The Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in the DRC clears 1,003 confirmed cases and 254 deaths as of 21 June 2026 — the second-largest Ebola outbreak on record — and a new medRxiv preprint from Okingo, Iroungou & Valdano quantifies introduction and sustained-transmission risk across DRC health zones to flag where the next chain is most likely. A Maputo Central Hospital severe-malaria cohort sequences Plasmodium falciparum from 149 adults and finds higher mortality plus an enriched K189T pfkelch artemisinin-resistance signal in HIV-coinfected patients. AbbVie agrees to acquire Apogee Therapeutics for $10.9B in cash for half-life-extended IL-13 antibody zumilokibart — a quarterly-to-semiannual challenger to Dupixent in atopic dermatitis. Insilico Medicine inks an up-to-$2.5B AI drug-discovery pact with SK Biopharmaceuticals on neuroimmune CNS targets at BIO 2026. biomeStat, a deterministic agentic-AI orchestrator from the Malavige group, runs end-to-end genomic epidemiology on 1,000 Asian dengue genomes in under 24 hours and flags 1,869 candidate immune-escape sites. And Heliconius butterflies live up to 348 days — ~25x their nearest relatives — with at least one species showing no grip-strength decline with age (Foley/STRI, Nature Communications).
item.itunes:summary: Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak in the DRC tops 1,003 confirmed cases and 254 deaths (WHO/DRC MoH, 21 June 2026); medRxiv preprint maps introduction and sustained-transmission risk across DRC health zones (medRxiv, 22 June 2026). Maputo Hospital Central severe-malaria sequencing study finds higher mortality and a K189T pfkelch enrichment in HIV-coinfected adults (medRxiv, 22 June 2026). AbbVie to acquire Apogee Therapeutics for $10.9B for half-life-extended IL-13 antibody zumilokibart, closing Q3 2026 (22 June 2026). Insilico Medicine and SK Biopharmaceuticals announce up-to-$2.5B AI drug-discovery collaboration on neuroimmune CNS targets at BIO 2026. biomeStat: deterministic agentic-AI orchestrator runs 1,000-Asian-dengue-genome epidemiology in <24h, flags 1,869 candidate immune-escape sites and conserved antiviral residues for JNJ-1802 and NITD-688 across all four DENV serotypes (bioRxiv, 23 June 2026). Heliconius butterflies live up to 348 days with little physical decline — Foley lab (Bristol) and STRI in Nature Communications.

item.title: Moderna mFlusiva mRNA flu wins FDA panel 9-0 + Australian XDR Shigella sonnei escalation + GENATATORs DNA language models for gene annotation + T. rex took 40 years to grow up
item.description: Moderna's mRNA-1010 flu vaccine (mFlusiva) clears FDA VRBPAC with unanimous 9-0 votes for adults 50-64 and 65+; PDUFA 5 August 2026. A 20-year Melbourne MDU-PHL genomic surveillance preprint tracks Shigella sonnei in Victoria from drug-sensitive through MDR to two distinct XDR sub-lineages, driving shifts in clinical management. GENATATORs benchmarks DNA language models on ab initio gene annotation and finds pretrained embeddings alone aren't enough — fine-tuning is essential, standard metrics miss what biologists actually care about. And a new PeerJ paper from Holly Woodward and colleagues across 17 specimens revises T. rex growth from ~25 to ~40 years.
item.itunes:summary: Moderna's mRNA-1010 (mFlusiva) wins unanimous FDA VRBPAC backing 9-0 in 50-64 and 65+ adults (18 June 2026; PDUFA 5 August 2026). MDU-PHL Melbourne 20-year genomic surveillance of Shigella sonnei in Victoria documents sensitive to MDR to two distinct XDR sub-lineages (bioRxiv, 20 June 2026). GENATATORs: fine-tuned DNA language models for gene annotation outperform existing tools and generalize across deep evolutionary distance, but pretraining alone is insufficient and standard metrics miss the point (bioRxiv, 21 June 2026). T. rex took roughly 40 years to reach full size — Woodward, Myhrvold & Horner in PeerJ (22 June 2026).

item.title: Bundibugyo Ebola PHEIC update + Bedford lab on saltational SARS-CoV-2 + Jazz/AbCellera $4B TCE pact + BioBrain agentic microscopy + Cordyceps-mimic spider
item.description: WHO June 19 update on the Bundibugyo virus outbreak (DRC 896/232, Uganda 19/2). Bedford lab paper across 4.4M genomes traces SARS-CoV-2 saltational events to persistent human infections, not zoonosis. Jazz Pharmaceuticals and AbCellera ink an up-to-$4B T-cell engager pact in solid tumors. BioBrain, a multi-agent framework for natural-language microscopy analysis, quietly outperforms frontier LLMs on quantitative reproducibility. And Taczanowskia waska, a new Ecuadorian spider that mimics the Cordyceps-like fungus that normally kills its kind.
item.itunes:summary: Bundibugyo Ebola PHEIC update (WHO DON, 19 June 2026); Bedford lab paper finds SARS-CoV-2 saltational events trace to persistent human infections (bioRxiv, 19 June 2026); Jazz/AbCellera up-to-$4B T-cell engager collaboration (June 17, 2026); BioBrain multi-agent microscopy analysis framework (bioRxiv, 21 June 2026); Taczanowskia waska, an Amazonian spider mimicking the parasitic fungus that infects spiders (Zootaxa, June 2026).
